channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Estas hembras marinas evolucionaron para lograrlo todo
item.description: Según un nuevo estudio, estos peces de aguas profundas acabaron utilizando señuelos brillantes no solo para pescar, sino también para atraer a sus parejas.
item.dc:creator: Kate Golembiewski

item.title: Reseña de ‘Margo tiene problemas de dinero’: te convertirá en un OnlyFan
item.description: Elle Fanning protagoniza una comedia dramática llena de corazón y mente abierta sobre la exposición en internet y sus complicaciones.
item.dc:creator: James Poniewozik

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 19, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Esta vez Lena Dunham decide quedarse en cama
item.description: En “Famesick”, su segundo libro de memorias, Dunham explora, con detalles ingeniosos y desgarradores, los estragos de la fama temprana y de vivir con una enfermedad crónica.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Egan

media:content.media:credit: Nina Westervelt para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lena Dunham y Andrew Rannells durante la parada de la gira del libro Famesick en la Academia de Música de Brooklyn.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Iran Declares Strait of Hormuz Closed Again as Ships Report Attacks
item.description: Iran reversed course on reopening the strategic waterway, warning it would continue to block transit as long as the U.S. blockade of ships from Iranian ports remained in effect.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:description: Ships and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of Musandam, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: You Can’t Game Your Way to a Real Education
item.description: Technology must return to its proper place in the classroom — as a supplemental tool rather than the source and summit of education.
item.dc:creator: Molly Worthen

item.title: The Mind-Altering Power of Lucid Dreaming
item.description: Few people experience lucid dreams, yet those who do are able to appreciate as much asleep as the rest of us do awake.
item.dc:creator: Cody Delistraty

item.title: Quote of the Day: After Artemis II, Astronauts and NASA Look to Moon Landing
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, April 19, 2026.

item.title: Is the Trend of Older Women in Fashion Here to Stay?
item.description: Representation of women of a certain age has never been higher. What’s going on?
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: What to Know About the Bulgarian Election
item.description: The Black Sea country is holding its eighth election in five years, with Bulgarians yearning for the kind of prosperous life enjoyed by other Europeans.
item.dc:creator: Carlotta Gall

media:content.media:description: Former President Rumen Radev of Bulgaria speaking on Thursday during the last pre-election event of his coalition, Progressive Bulgaria. Most polls show the coalition leading, but not necessarily with enough support for a majority.

item.title: Hezbollah is willing to cooperate with a cease-fire with Israel. For now.
item.description: The leader of the Iran-backed militia said that a more durable peace with Israel would require the fulfillment of a list of long-standing demands.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Protesters waved an Iranian flag and flags for Hezbollah in downtown Beirut during a demonstration to show support for Hezbollah last week.

item.title: Universal Genève Returns to the Watch World With New Models
item.description: The brand has undergone a two-year rebuilding after it was acquired by Breitling in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: Watches and Wonders Geneva: A Week in Time
item.description: A look at what’s new at the Geneva fair.
item.dc:creator: Robin Swithinbank

item.title: At Tiffany, Watch Design Inspired by Its Own Jewelry Icons
item.description: The house has translated its paillonné enamel bangle, once favored by Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis, into a limited-edition timepiece.
item.dc:creator: Milena Lazazzera

item.title: Humans Who Used a Bear Suit to Defraud Car Insurers Sentenced to Jail
item.description: The California residents collected more than $141,000 in insurance payouts after staging bear attacks on their luxury cars, state officials said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:description: A California Department of Fish and Wildlife biologist who reviewed video footage of the attacks on luxury cars said that the animal was “clearly a human in a bear suit,” the California Department of Insurance said.

item.title: Potential 2028 Democrats Audition in Michigan,With a Focus on Trump
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris, Senator Cory Booker of New Jersey and Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky spoke at a gathering of party insiders in Detroit, fueling presidential speculation.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Steve Friess

media:content.media:credit: Nick Hagen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris addresses the crowd at the Michigan Democratic Women’s Caucus Legacy Luncheon in Detroit on Saturday.

item.title: These Watches Are Personal, Unique and Made to Keep
item.description: For the ultimate in high-level luxury, buyers are commissioning timepieces that are special enough to be passed down through generations.
item.dc:creator: Carol Besler

item.title: U.S. Installs a Trump Loyalist to Lead ‘Grand Conspiracy’ Case Into Trump Foes
item.description: A former lawyer for President Trump’s campaign, Joseph diGenova, is said to be planning to split time between Miami and Fort Pierce, where a grand jury overseen by a Trump-favored judge sits.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage and Alan Feuer
item.category: Russian Interference in 2016 US Elections and Ties to Trump Associates

media:content.media:description: Joseph diGenova, 81, who served as a U.S. attorney in the Reagan era, has been given the title of counselor to the attorney general and detailed to the Southern District of Florida.

item.title: Activists Tear-Gassed at Failed Raid of Beagle Research Facility
item.description: Some 1,000 protesters tried to storm a private breeding and lab facility in Wisconsin in an effort to steal thousands of beagles that are bred for medical experimentation.
item.dc:creator: Katie Benner and Taylor Glascock

media:content.media:credit: Taylor Glascock for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Animal rights activists and the police face off outside a research facility in Blue Mounds, Wis., on Saturday.

item.title: Funcionarios de EE. UU. viajaron a La Habana para discutir reformas en Cuba
item.description: La delegación les dijo a los líderes de Cuba que solo tenían un estrecho margen de tiempo para realizar los cambios económicos y políticos exigidos por el gobierno de Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

media:content.media:description: Una calle de La Habana el mes pasado. Durante las conversaciones de la semana pasada, según dijo un funcionario del Departamento de Estado, la delegación estadounidense insistió en que el presidente Trump estaba abierto a alcanzar una solución diplomática a la crisis.

item.title: Nuclear Fusion
item.description: Michael Lieberman brings us a Sunday puzzle with some things different (and some things the same).
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:description: Command + C for Cute, Command + V for Variation.

item.title: Iran Says the Strait of Hormuz Is Closed Again
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards said they were closing the Strait of Hormuz until the U.S. blockade was lifted. Two Indian-flagged ships reported being hit as they tried to cross the passage.
item.dc:creator: McKinnon de Kuyper
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Wisconsin’s Chazen Museum of Art Explores New Ways to Display Its Collection
item.description: At the Chazen Museum of Art in Wisconsin, each gallery will now have a single “focus object,” with “a constellation of other artworks” helping to draw out particular themes.
item.dc:creator: Ted Loos

item.title: Man Charged in Lego Theft Scheme of Replacing Pieces With Pasta, Police Say
item.description: A California man was charged with grand theft after the police said he reaped about $34,000 in what an official called an “off the charts” pasta-and-switch scheme involving Lego kits.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:description: The police said a California man bought Lego sets to orchestrate a refund scheme that involved bags of pasta.

item.title: After the Heists: Securing Museums Without Closing Them Off
item.description: The bold robbery at the Louvre shocked the world, but all museums face the challenge of protecting art and historical treasures.
item.dc:creator: Sam Lubell

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A variety of new walls step along the sloping site of the Norton Simon Museum, including a textured white concrete barrier in various segments, to the east and a matte white wall to the west.

item.title: Pope Leo Says News Outlets Misunderstood Recent Remarks as Trump Criticism
item.description: Pope Leo XIV said that news outlets misinterpreted his recent remarks as criticism of President Trump. The pontiff has called for an end to the violence in the Middle East but told reporters on Saturday he had no interest in debating the president.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Mitssunaga

media:content.media:description: El papa León XIV en Yaundé, capital de Camerún, el sábado.

item.title: U.S. Officials Visited Havana to Lay Out Proposals for Cuban Reforms
item.description: The delegation told Cuba’s leadership that it had only a narrow window of time to make the economic and political changes demanded by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega and Zolan Kanno-Youngs

media:content.media:description: A street in Havana last month. During the talks last week, a State Department official said, the American delegation insisted that President Trump was open to reaching a diplomatic solution to the crisis.

item.title: Mamdani and Obama Lead Preschool Singalong to Promote Free Child Care
item.description: In their first public appearance together, Mayor Zohran Mamdani and former President Barack Obama met up for some joking and singing on Saturday at a Bronx child care center.
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:description: The meeting between the two men came as Mayor Zohran Mamdani was seeking to expand free child care for 2-year-olds.

item.title: Hollywood Talent Giant Draws Suitors Amid Epstein Fallout
item.description: Formerly known as Wasserman, the agency that has changed its name to The Team put itself up for sale after its founder’s emails surfaced in the Epstein files.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Mullin and Lauren Hirsch

media:content.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Casey Wasserman last year. He announced in February that he would be exploring a sale of the talent agency he founded.

item.title: El papa dice que los medios malinterpretaron algunos de sus comentarios como críticas a Trump
item.description: El papa León respondió directamente el lunes a un ataque presidencial. Pero desde entonces, dijo, se han malinterpretado algunas de sus declaraciones durante su viaje a África.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El papa León XIV en Yaundé, capital de Camerún, el sábado.

item.title: How to Give Yourself a Sun-Kissed Glow
item.description: The singer Victoria Monét, the former Miss World Lisa Hanna and the makeup artist Sir John share their body makeup tips.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Coates

item.title: Kyiv Mass Shooting Kills 6 and Gunman Is Shot Dead After Taking Hostages in Grocery
item.description: It was the deadliest mass shooting in Ukraine in years, where firearms have proliferated since the war with Russia began.
item.dc:creator: Constant Méheut

media:content.media:description: Outside the supermarket in Kyiv, Ukraine, where a man took hostages and was killed by the police on Saturday.

item.title: Remains Linked to Submerged Car Solve 1958 Mystery of Missing Oregon Family
item.description: The case of the missing Martin family was unsolved until a diver found a car in an Oregon river in 2024. Officials, relying on DNA tests, said Thursday that they had identified the remains of three people.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk

media:content.media:credit: Beth Nakamura/The Oregonian, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Remains recovered from an Oregon river in 2025 were identified as three family members who went missing in 1958, the authorities said on Thursday. The bodies of two other family members had been found in 1959.

item.title: Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz as Iran Declares Strict Control of Vital Shipping Route
item.description: The day after Iran declared the vital waterway open, it reversed course, injecting new peril into navigation there.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: Tankers anchored in the Strait of Hormuz on Saturday.

item.title: Six Are Missing After Cargo Ship Is Found Overturned Near Guam
item.description: The ship, the Mariana, was found overturned days after the Coast Guard lost contact with its crew, after a super typhoon struck.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: U.S. Coast Guard, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: The U.S. Coast Guard is searching for six crew members from the Mariana, a U.S.-registered dry cargo vessel, which was found overturned in the Pacific.

item.title: A Dance Party for Martha Graham With Ariana DeBose and Padma Lakshmi
item.description: Stars and luminaries honored the dance company’s 100th anniversary with dinner, performances and a champagne toast at the New York Public Library.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr and Rebecca Smeyne
item.category: Graham, Martha, Center of Contemporary Dance
New York Public Library for the Performing Arts

item.title: Trump Is Fixated on 1896
item.description: The president loves the 19th century. But why?
item.dc:creator: Jamelle Bouie

media:content.media:credit: Paola Chapdelaine for The New York Times

item.title: Paul Waldman, Creator and Curator of Art Museum for Birds, Dies at 89
item.description: A dyslexic teenager, he reinvented himself as a bodybuilder. Then he turned to art, producing transgressive paintings and elaborate birdhouses.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald

media:content.media:credit: Michael Shavel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Paul Waldman in 1993 with the International Bird Museum that he built on the roof of his house in Southampton, N.Y. The multistory birdhouse had an art gallery that displayed miniature works by Roy Lichtenstein and others.

item.title: A Big Beautiful Chopped Salad
item.description: All the hits are here: salty bacon, creamy avocado, funky blue cheese, sweet tomato, crisp romaine.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: Bobbi Lin for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Eugene Jho. Prop Stylist: Christina Lane.
media:content.media:description: Melissa Clark’s chopped salad.

item.title: More Displaced Lebanese Head Home as Cease-Fire Is Tested
item.description: The truce appeared to mostly hold even as Israel said it had carried out strikes on what it called “terrorists” approaching its forces in southern Lebanon. Separately, a U.N. peacekeeper was killed.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Sarah Chaayto and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Displaced Lebanese residents returned to their homes in the south on Saturday.

item.title: Why Your Paycheck Feels Smaller
item.description: Ben Casselman, our chief economics correspondent, explains why wages are not keeping up with inflation and what that means for American workers and the economy.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman, Nour Idriss, Sutton Raphael and Stephanie Swart

item.title: How Cheap Drones Are Changing Wars Like the Ones in Ukraine and Iran
item.description: The United States and its allies have historically prioritized accurate but expensive defense solutions. Drones challenge that math.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Cai, Jeremy White and John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Jeremy White and Ashley Cai/The New York Times

item.title: Pope Says News Outlets Misread Some of His Remarks as Criticism of Trump
item.description: Pope Leo responded directly on Monday to a presidential attack. But since then, he said, some of his statements during his Africa trip have been misconstrued.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:description: El papa León XIV en Yaundé, capital de Camerún, el sábado.

item.title: Appeals Court Again Allows Ballroom Construction to Go On, for Now
item.description: In the latest ruling, an appeals court in Washington allowed construction to continue until at least June while it considered the case.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An appeals court in Washington allowed construction of President Trump’s ballroom to continue into June.

item.title: Three Perfect and Riffable Brownie Recipes
item.description: Fudgy, freezer-friendly brownies are easier than cake and vanish just as fast.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Kelly Marshall for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Samantha Seneviratne.
media:content.media:description: Vaughn Vreeland’s triple-chocolate brownies.

item.title: In Trump’s Orbit, Women Aren’t the Only Ones Concerned About Their Looks
item.description: In Trump world, the male ego is often evident — and their appearance scrutinized — under a president’s gaze.
item.dc:creator: Jesse McKinley

media:content.media:description: For all the talk about Mar-a-Lago-inspired cosmetic surgery for women in Trump’s world, the attention paid to, and the efforts to safeguard, the male ego also stand out. Many of the men in his administration have adopted a signature look.

item.title: Corporate Profits Are at Record Highs. These 4 Factors Could Sink Them.
item.description: Experts have different theories about what’s driving steep gains. But many agree the window for growth is shrinking.
item.dc:creator: Peter Coy

media:content.media:description: An Amazon data center being built in New Carlisle, Ind.

item.title: Why the Stock Market Makes No Sense Right Now
item.description: It will always be saved.
item.dc:creator: Kyla Scanlon

item.title: The Four Lessons Liberals Should Consider After Orban’s Defeat
item.description: The lesson for Republicans comes too late, but liberals can still learn from the end of Orbanism.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat

media:content.media:credit: András Zoltai for The New York Times

item.title: JD Vance’s Very Bad Week
item.description: What recent events in Europe can tell us about the future of Trumpism.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle, David French, Michelle Goldberg, Derek Arthur and Jillian Weinberger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; photograph by Attila Kisbenedek
